pi_items.source: SEM magnification calculation: 600 pixels × 0.49 µm/pixel = 294 µm field of view, consistent with ~20,000x SEM magnification typical for solder microstructure analysis
Standard SI unit convention for micrometer scale measurements in materials science
Standard threshold for statistical significance in microstructural analysis; consistent with materials science practice for quantitative metallography
Reasonable minimum for detecting a specific phase in multi-phase Au-Pb-Sn eutectic solder microstructure
Plausible maximum for bright phase (intermetallic AuSn4) content in Au-Pb-Sn eutectic solder; consistent with documented intermetallic phase distributions in ternary solder systems
Pixel conversion: 0.5 µm² ÷ (0.49 µm/pixel)² ≈ 2 pixels², at practical detection limit for SEM analysis
Pixel conversion: 500 µm² ÷ (0.49 µm/pixel)² ≈ 2,080 pixels², well within image bounds (600×412 = 247,200 pixels); consistent with large intermetallic particles in solder microstructures
Path matches directory structure created in setup_task.sh (mkdir -p /home/ga/Fiji_Data/results/sem_analysis); follows standard ImageJ particle analysis output format
Path matches directory structure created in setup_task.sh; standard location for segmentation visualization outputs
Path matches directory structure created in setup_task.sh; standard location for analysis summary reports
Image downloaded and verified to exist in official ImageJ repository (141KB, JPEG 600×412 pixels grayscale, last modified 2017-01-14); setup_task.sh attempts to download from multiple mirrors

Documented in scale_info.txt file created by setup_task.sh; Au-Pb-Sn ternary eutectic is a real material system used in microelectronics packaging (verified through research literature on solder microstructures)
Specified in scale_info.txt: 'Instrument: SEM, Backscatter Electron (BSE) detector'; BSE is standard for phase contrast analysis in solder microstructures
Specified in task description and scale_info.txt BSE Contrast Key; bright phase in BSE corresponds to high atomic number elements (Au/Au-Sn compounds)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real AuPbSn40 SEM micrograph (600×412 pixels, grayscale) from the official ImageJ image repository (https://imagej.net/images/AuPbSn40.jpg). The image shows a real Au-Pb-Sn eutectic solder microstructure acquired with ~20,000x SEM magnification (pixel size 0.49 µm/pixel consistent with 294 µm field of view). The task requires analysis of the bright phase (AuSn4 intermetallics) using BSE contrast. Expected particle count ≥15, area fraction 1-60%, and mean particle area 0.5-500 µm² are all physically reasonable ranges for this solder system. Output files (CSV, overlay image, summary text) follow standard ImageJ/Fiji analysis conventions. All metadata claims regarding calibration, expected ranges, and output paths are consistent with real SEM microstructure analysis practices.
